name: AI-Powered Jira Test Plan and Test Case Generation

options.systemMessage: You are an expert QA analyst. Analyze bug tickets thoroughly to identify testing requirements, affected areas, and risks.
You are an expert test plan writer. Create detailed, professional test plans following industry best practices and IEEE 829 standards.

parameters.text:
=Generate 15-25 detailed test cases for this bug based on the test plan.

Ticket: {{ $("Extract Ticket Data").item.json.ticketKey }}
Summary: {{ $("Extract Ticket Data").item.json.summary }}

Test Plan Summary:
{{ $("AI Test Plan Writer").item.json.output }}

Generate test cases covering:
1. Positive scenarios (happy path)
2. Negative scenarios (error handling)
3. Edge cases (boundary conditions)
4. Regression scenarios (ensure no side effects)
5. Cross-device compatibility
6. Performance scenarios

Each test case must include:
- testCaseId (unique ID like TC001, TC002, etc.)
- title (clear, concise test case name)
- description (what is being tested)
- preconditions (setup required)
- testSteps (numbered steps to execute)
- expectedResult (what should happen)
- testType (Functional, UI, Regression, etc.)
- priority (High, Medium, Low)
- severity (Critical, High, Medium, Low)

options.systemMessage: You are an expert test case designer. Create comprehensive, detailed test cases that cover all scenarios including positive, negative, edge cases, and regression testing.

parameters.subject: =Test Plan and Cases for {{ $("Extract Ticket Data").item.json.ticketKey }}: {{ $("Extract Ticket Data").item.json.summary }}
parameters.message:
=

Test Plan Generated for {{ $("Extract Ticket Data").item.json.ticketKey }}

Summary: {{ $("Extract Ticket Data").item.json.summary }}

Priority: {{ $("Extract Ticket Data").item.json.priority }}

Test Plan

{{ $("AI Test Plan Writer").item.json.output }}

Test Cases

{{ $("AI Test Case Generator").item.json.output.testCases.length }} test cases have been generated and uploaded to Google Drive.

View Test Cases in Google Drive

The Excel file contains all detailed test cases ready for execution.

parameters.text:
=# Generate jira ticket based on following inforamtion jira fields.

{{ $json.body.summary }}

{{ $json.body.description }}

# Based on summary and descriptionn you need to provide add "Steps to reproduce:", "Expected result:", "Actual Result:"

{{ $json.body.priority }} "numeric_id_here" 

# Analyze the incoming bug data and return a JSON object. You must map the priority to the following numeric IDs:

If the priority is Blocker, return 1.

If the priority is High, return 2.

If the priority is Medium, return 3.

If the priority is Low, return 4.

{{ $json.body.screenshotPath }}

# Add Screenshot details

options.systemMessage: You are a bug report formatter. Analyze the user's bug report and extract: summary (brief title), description (detailed explanation), priority (Low/Medium/High based on severity), and issueType (always "Bug"). Return structured JSON.

parameters.content: # Jira bug fetch and prepare TestPlan and TestCases

# **REAL LIVE Project( Multi Agent System) - Test Plan, Writer, Bug -> Triaging. **
[﻿bugzz.atlassian.net/browse/VWO-48](https://bugzz.atlassian.net/browse/VWO-48) 

Output : A professional Test Plan document for the Bug Occured (.docx)

Pipeline:

1. JiraFetcherTool → fetches ticket from Jira REST API
2. Test Plan Analyst → analyzes the ticket & identifies scope
3. Test Plan Writer → writes the full test plan using a template,  the severity, priority, and let us know how important this bug is 


> JIRA Ticket -> Test Plan, TestCases -> Excel File (Templates which are given by us)



Setup your Own JIRA for the token - 

[﻿www.loom.com/share/47e8ed337bce40b285364c8aab349701](https://www.loom.com/share/47e8ed337bce40b285364c8aab349701) 

Test Plan Template. - [﻿https://docs.google.com/document/d/1JoeTJRM2-D6RBtPCpztX3OYH9apewHLb7f5jarCCVkg/edit?usp=sharing](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1JoeTJRM2-D6RBtPCpztX3OYH9apewHLb7f5jarCCVkg/edit?usp=sharing) 

All Templates - 

[﻿https://sdet.live/project1](https://sdet.live/project1)
